dna.borrowedTrait: two-column rhythm with text-left / image-right alternation in the doctor-intro section
dna.divergenceRationale: Both recent warm-family builds used warm-editorial with a split-image hero — repeating that combination here would produce a near-identical visual skeleton despite the different palette. Card-heavy with an asymmetric-left hero shifts the structural grammar entirely: the hero anchors copy in the bottom-left over a full-bleed warm image rather than halving the viewport, and the services section becomes a scannable card grid that foregrounds sedation and special-care positioning rather than editorial rows.
dna.creativeDirection: Warm terracotta cards on a cream field, grounded by Lora headings at a measured scale — the site should feel like a well-lit, unhurried reception space rather than a clinical brochure, letting the practice's genuine clinical depth surface through quiet layout confidence rather than credential density.
dna.writingTone: Warm, clear, specific to Fountain Valley.
dna.brandSummary: Elements Dentistry, led by Dr. Tiffany Vo, serving Fountain Valley, — Warm terracotta cards on a cream field, grounded by Lora headings at a measured scale — the site should feel like a well-lit, unhurried reception space rather than a clinical brochure, letting the practice's genuine clinical depth surface through quiet layout confidence rather than credential density.
doRules: Use the archetype's established card-heavy layout — don't mix incompatible grid styles mid-page.
Hero variant must be asymmetric-left — lock this choice.
Card treatment is soft-shadow — apply consistently across all card instances.
Phone number must be a tel: link in the mobile header, minimum 44px touch target.
Doctor name and credentials must appear without scrolling on the homepage.
Primary CTA copy must reference a specific action and ideally the doctor or city.
dontRules: No gradient text (background-clip: text) — AI-slop tell #1.
No faux-glass / backdrop-filter cards unless archetype explicitly calls for it.
No centered-headline + two-CTA + microlinks hero — the most overused AI pattern.
No stat-bar section if yearsExperience/happyPatients data is missing — render empty = fail.
No stock photography of smiling model families.

No generic, boilerplate, or template-sounding copy. Every headline, tagline, and CTA must reference this specific practice, doctor, or city — never industry-generic phrasing that could appear on any competitor site.
No archetype+hero combo already used in recent own-builds.
No section that renders with dashes, nulls, or placeholder text visible to users.
dna.evalRationale: Candidate 2's creative direction is the most specific and evocative — 'well-lit, unhurried reception space rather than a clinical brochure' is a concrete, unusual framing that distinguishes it from the generic credential-forward language in Candidates 0 and 1, and its divergence rationale most precisely articulates the structural grammar shift (full-bleed hero vs. halved viewport) rather than restating the same points less crisply.
